Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»When are Team SA in Olympics action on Wednesday?

A host of Team SA athletes will be in action at the Olympics on Wednesday. Here are all the times to take note of so you don’t miss a thing.

On Wednesday, there will once again be plenty of attention on the exploits of Team SA’s first gold medallist, Tatjana Smith, who returns to the pool.

The swimming superstarwill be hoping to take a few laps towards another gold medalas she takes part in the heats and – almost certainly – the200m breaststrokesemi-finals.

Team SA start Wednesday with three Olympics medals, with the men’s sevens rugby bronze medal and Alan Hatherly (bronze) added to Smith’s gold lifting them to 12th place on the overall table

ARCHERY

Men’s individual 1/32 limination round (7.16pm):Wiaan Roux vs Je Deok Kim (Kor)

HOCKEY

Women’s Pool B (10.30am):Team SA vs Great Britain

Men’s Pool A (7.45pm):Team SA vs Spain

ROWING

Women’s singles scull, semi-finals C/D (10.24am):Paige Badenhorst. Note: Badenhorst is competing for 13th to 24th position

Men’s pair, semi-final A/B (10.34am):John Smith and Christoher Baxter. How it works: There are two semi-finals of six boats. The first three in each go into final A and the remainder into final B. The medals come from final A

SWIMMING

Women’s 200m breaststroke heats (11am):Tatjana Smith, Kaylene Corbett

Men’s 200m backstroke, heats (11.27am):Pieter Coetze

Men’s 200m backstroke, semi-finals (9.47pm):Pieter Coetze

Women’s 200m breaststroke semi-finals (10.03pm):Tatjana Smith, Kaylene Corbett

TRIATHLON

Women’s individual (8am):Vicky van der Merwe

Men’s individual (10.45am):Henri Schoeman and James Riddle

Compiled byTeam SA websitein Paris

TUESDAY, 30 JULY

CYCLING (BMX)

Men’s park qualificationVincent Leygonie was unable to finish in the top 9 and was eliminated after placing 12th with a points average of 75.85 after two runs

HOCKEY

Men’s Pool A:Team SA 1 Germany 5

ROWING

Women’s single sculls, quarter-finals:Paige Bandenhorst finished fourth in her quarter-final. How it works: The first three from each of the four heats progress to semi-finals A/B. The remainder go through to semi-finals C/D. Badenhorst therefore will be competing for places 13th to 24th.

RUGBY SEVENS

Women’s 11th/12th pace playoff: Team SA 21 Fiji 15

SURFING

Women’s third round: POSTPONEDSarah Baum vs Clarissa Moore

SWIMMING

Men’s 200m butterfly, heats:Matthew Sates finished 6th in 1:57.04. That p[aced him 20th on the overall time sheets and he missed out on a place in the semi-finals.

TRIATHLON

Men’s Individual: POSTPONEDJamie Riddle and Henri Schoeman. The event will now be held on Wednesday
